lines.line: > The speaker was born in Harlem, New York City, and moved to Washington Heights at the age of three. lines.line: # Family Background and Emphasis on Education

> The speaker delves into their family's history, including their parents' migration from the Caribbean to New York during World War I. They highlight the hard work of their parents and the lack of overt expressions of love, but a strong emphasis on the importance of education as a means of securing a future.

story.title: Sedrick Rawlins and Alyce Rawling 07062017
story.description: The summary of the interview with Dr. Sedric J. Rollins reflects a rich narrative of his life, encompassing his childhood in Harlem and Washington Heights, New York, education, military service, and his career in dentistry. Dr. Rollins discusses his journey through prestigious institutions like Lincoln University and Meharry Medical College, his experiences in the military as a pre-aviation cadet during WWII, and his transition into dentistry. The interview also highlights the systemic racism he faced, including challenges in securing housing and business loans, which fueled his active involvement with the NAACP. His contributions to the community, along with reflections on personal and professional milestones, paint a vivid picture of resilience and advocacy.
